text.title: inc-read-only-investigation-default Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for inc-read-only-investigation-default. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: The SRE agent's persistent ServiceAccount MUST have only the *-readonly role bound at boot. Mutation verbs (patch, delete, create, update) MUST live in a separate role that is unbound by default. The agent MUST NOT receive write credentials via static kubeconfig, long-lived secrets, or environment variables.
rule.rationale: If write credentials exist on the agent's host, prompt safety is irrelevant — any process compromise grants full privilege. A second binding controlled by a separate workflow is the only durable enforcement.

rule.statement: Trust escalation MUST require an explicit /agent escalate-trust <incident_id> invocation by the verified on-caller, MUST require fresh MFA proof issued within the last 60 seconds, MUST create a remediate RoleBinding with TTL of 60 minutes or less, and MUST auto-revoke on whichever fires first: incident-close webhook or TTL deadline. Escalations MUST NOT outlive their incident.
rule.rationale: The most common postmortem finding for agent-related incidents is "the elevation never expired". A self-revoking binding makes the leak structurally impossible; MFA binds the elevation to a specific human at a specific moment for the audit log.
